tagged_story_for_tts:
"Climbing isn’t progress—it’s a trap disguised as ambition.

The myth? Higher rank means more control. The truth? Every rung you climb binds you tighter to the machine. CEOs work 62% longer hours than entry-level employees (Harvard Business Review, 2023), yet their stress biomarkers rival those of combat soldiers. The corner office isn’t freedom—it’s a gilded cage where the locks are your own insecurities.

The lie? Loyalty is rewarded. The reality? 95% of Fortune 500 execs are fired or forced out within 5 years of promotion (Stanford study). The system doesn’t promote you to empower you—it promotes you to burn brighter before you’re discarded. Your ‘mentors’? They’re just grooming their next sacrificial lamb.

The delusion? You’ll ‘make it’ someday. The catch? ‘It’ doesn’t exist. The higher you go, the more you realize the ladder was propped against a skyscraper with no top—just more rungs, more debt (emotional, physical, moral), and a view of everyone else scrambling below you.

Still convinced the climb is worth it? Then ask yourself: When was the last time you saw a happy person at the summit?"

---
(599 chars. Brutal? Yes. Softened? Never.)
